intro: Migrating from C to TypeScript bridges low-level procedural systems programming with modern, safe, type-annotated application development. Developers gain structural typing boundaries and compile-time linting safety, shielding codebases from the unpredictable runtime surprises common in plain JavaScript. This transition is typical when upgrading embedded command tooling or system configuration utilities to cross-platform cloud and enterprise node architectures.

diffs.from: Statically typed, low-level architecture with no runtime bounds checking, permitting arbitrary pointer type casting.
diffs.to: Statically typed via structural shapes at compile time, which are entirely erased when compiled to runtime JavaScript.

diffs.from: Procedural syntax demanding separate system header mappings, explicit structs, and explicit numeric boundaries.
diffs.to: Modern application layout syntax supporting interfaces, types, explicit classes, decorators, and modular import statements.

diffs.from: Manually handled via explicit stack placement or precise dynamic heap allocation and deallocation code.
diffs.to: Automatically managed by the host runtime engine's tracking garbage collector, freeing developers from memory operations.

diffs.from: Dependent entirely on underlying native OS threading configurations and low-level pipeline blocks.
diffs.to: Single-threaded non-blocking execution runtime driven by Promises, microtask tracking, and async/await syntax structures.

diffs.from: Minimalist library managing platform math, raw string calculations, files, and core memory streams.
diffs.to: Extensive platform ecosystem providing runtime utility objects, collection operations, web interfaces, and ecosystem modules.

examples.title: Structural Contract Layouts
examples.description: Refactoring rigid C structure maps into scalable compile-time TypeScript type definitions and interfaces.

examples.title: Array Filtering and Transformations
examples.description: Converting manual C element loop transformations into concise, safe TypeScript functional array operations.

tips.title: Lean on Structural Matching
tips.body: TypeScript verifies objects by their shape rather than explicitly registered names; objects matching an interface layout pass validations automatically.

tips.title: Configure Strict Compilation Rules
tips.body: Ensure 'strict': true is active in your tsconfig file to catch dangerous null or missing property states during development.

tips.title: Ditch Header Declarations
tips.body: Organize your application logic using modern ES modules (import/export statements) instead of dragging in file fragments using header includes.

tips.title: Avoid Arbitrary Any Annotations
tips.body: Do not bypass type checking using the 'any' keyword. Use 'unknown' or explicit union configurations when data inputs are volatile.
